    non_exhaustive: false,
    docstring: Some(
        "Every bindable filter-field value (`nmp_grammar::Binding` mirror).\nRecursive through `FfiDerived`/`FfiSetOp` -- both are UniFFI **objects**\n(`Arc`-handles), not records: UniFFI's proc-macro mode lowers/lifts\n`Arc<T>` only when `T` is itself an exported object (an opaque handle),\nnever a plain `Box<T>`/`Arc<T>`-wrapped record -- and a record directly\ncontaining itself is a compile-time infinite-size error in Rust regardless\nof UniFFI. Making the recursive point an object (constructor + getters,\nsee each type's `impl`) is the standard UniFFI idiom for a recursive value\ntype; it mirrors `nmp_grammar::Binding`'s own `Box<Derived>`/`Box<SetOp>`\nindirection, just via an opaque handle instead of a boxed value. `SetOp`'s\n`operands: Vec<FfiBinding>` needs no such indirection -- `Vec` is already\nheap-allocated, breaking the cycle on its own.",
    ),
}

    ),

            docstring: None,

That guarantee moved to\n`nmp-engine::core::EngineCore::on_publish`'s acceptance boundary\n(Unit A0/#56) so it holds for every entry point, not only this one;\nit surfaces on the `WriteStatus` receipt stream as `Failed` instead.",

This\nis the signer-side engine-attach failure; the other reachable\n`add_signer` outcome, `EngineError::EngineClosed` (engine shut down\nmid-handshake), is surfaced as `Disconnected` instead so the two are\nnot conflated. It is not a `Nip46Error` variant; it crosses a different\ninternal taxonomy (`nmp::EngineError`) at the same observer seam.",
            ),
        },

            ),
        },
    ],
    shape: Enum,
    non_exhaustive: false,
    docstring: Some(
        "`nmp_signer::Nip46Error` mirror (#494) -- every live discriminant a NIP-46\nconnection attempt can fail with, so a native caller can branch on\n\"auth required\" vs. \"timeout\" vs. \"malformed\" instead of parsing English.\n`Nip46Error::InvalidRelay`/`InvalidInvitation`/`SecretMismatch` are not\nmirrored: nothing in the workspace ever constructs them (see that type's\nown doc).",
    ),
}

        "A decoded public NIP-19 nostr entity (#116, `nmp::NostrEntity` mirror).\nEach variant carries EXACTLY the fields NIP-19 defines for that entity --\nnever force-fit into one shared shape: `npub`/`note` carry no relay\nhints at all (the format has none to carry); `nevent`'s `author`/`kind`\nare independently optional metadata; `naddr`'s `kind`/`author`/\n`identifier` are ALL required by the format, unlike `nevent`'s. There is\ndeliberately no `nsec`/`ncryptsec` variant here -- see\n`convert::decode_nostr_entity`'s doc for why a secret-key entity is\nrefused rather than decoded.",
    ),
}

enum Enum {

                    type_: UInt64,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "kind",
                    type_: UInt16,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "tags",
                    type_: Sequence {
                        inner_type: Sequence {
                            inner_type: String,
                        },
                    },
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "content",
                    type_: String,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
                Field {
                    name: "sig",
                    type_: String,
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
            ],
            docstring: None,
        },
    ],
    shape: Enum,
    non_exhaustive: false,
    docstring: Some(
        "The event payload of a write intent (`nmp::WritePayload` mirror). VISION\nP: signing and publishing are ORTHOGONAL stages -- `Unsigned` is a\ntemplate the engine signs internally (\"the key lives in the engine\",\nledger #12); `Signed` (#32, the M5 unlock) is a caller that already\nholds a validly-signed event -- an external signer / NIP-46 bunker, or a\nverbatim republish -- and hands its fields across as-is. `Signed`'s\nfields are field-for-field [`FfiRow`] (the read-side mirror of a signed\n`nostr::Event`) plus `sig`, deliberately: the write side stays symmetric\nwith the read side rather than introducing a JSON-blob shape.\n\n`Signed`'s fields are PARSED at this FFI boundary (typed hex/signature-\nshape errors, see `convert::signed_event_from_ffi`) but NOT verified\nhere (#52 Unit B) -- `nostr::Event::verify` runs at\n`nmp-engine::core::EngineCore::on_publish`'s acceptance boundary (Unit\nA0/#56) instead, so the guarantee holds for every entry point, not only\nthis one. A tampered `Signed` event still parses fine here and is\nrejected downstream, surfacing as `WriteStatus::Failed` on the receipt\nstream rather than a synchronous `FfiError`. The engine itself never\nre-signs, mutates a tag, or recomputes an id for this variant.",
    ),
}

enum Enum {
    name: "FfiWriteRouting",
    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
    remote: false,
    discr_type: None,
    variants: [
        Variant {
            name: "AuthorOutbox",
            discr: None,
            fields: [],
            docstring: None,
        },
        Variant {
            name: "ToInboxes",
            discr: None,
            fields: [
                Field {
                    name: "recipients",
                    type_: Sequence {
                        inner_type: String,
                    },
                    default: None,
                    docstring: None,
                },
            ],
            docstring: None,
        },
    ],
    shape: Enum,
    non_exhaustive: false,
    docstring: Some(
        "`nmp::WriteRouting` mirror -- `PrivateNarrow` deliberately has NO wire\nform here (#22/#52). `nmp_grammar::NarrowOnly::new`'s\nown doc requires \"the caller must already have resolved and narrowed\nthis itself\" -- i.e. a trusted protocol module's own logic, not a raw\nrelay-URL string handed across the FFI boundary by an app with no way to\nprove those URLs are actually private. Minting `PrivateRoute` from\nFFI-supplied strings would be exactly the \"raw app-provided expanded\nrelay set\"/\"route escape hatch\" #22's canonical design rules out; the\n`nmp` facade itself withholds re-exporting `NarrowOnly`/`PrivateRoute`\nfor the identical reason (see `crates/nmp/src/lib.rs`'s doc). A\nvalidated, opaque private-route mint belongs in a protocol module built\non direct Rust, not this FFI surface -- `AuthorOutbox`/`ToInboxes`\nremain the only FFI-constructible routing choices for now.\n\n#115: `PinnedHost(HostAuthority)` gets the IDENTICAL treatment, for the\nIDENTICAL reason -- this enum deliberately gains NO variant for it, and\nno `HostAuthority` constructor is exported anywhere in this crate. An\napp can only ever obtain a pinned-host write transitively, through a\nprotocol module's already-composed intent (`NmpEngine::group_message_intent`\n-> the opaque `FfiComposedWriteIntent`) -- never by naming a host\nitself. This exhaustive two-variant match IS the enforcement: a new\n`WriteRouting` variant landing in `nmp-grammar` without a corresponding\nFfiWriteRouting decision is a compile error here, not a silent gap.",
    ),
}

Retained, not\nterminal: this fact re-arrives verbatim on restart replay, and only\nregistering a signer for THIS exact pubkey resumes the write --\nnever a different (even currently-active) identity.",

record Record {
    name: "FfiFrame",
    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
    remote: false,
    fields: [
        Field {
            name: "deltas",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: Enum {
                    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                    name: "FfiRowDelta",
                },
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: Some(
                "Unbounded observations: the exact delta transition. ALWAYS empty for\nwindowed observations (see the type doc).",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "window",
            type_: Optional {
                inner_type: Record {
                    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                    name: "FfiWindowContents",
                },
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: Some(
                "Present iff the observation is windowed: the complete bounded row set\nplus the window's growth fact.",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "evidence",
            type_: Record {
                module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                name: "FfiAcquisitionEvidence",
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
    ],
    docstring: Some(
        "One delivered observation frame (`nmp::Frame` mirror) -- the ONE\nvocabulary both observation modes share. Delivery is DERIVED from\nboundedness, never a knob, and never carried twice on the wire:\n\n- Unbounded (`window` is `None`): `deltas` is the exact transition rebased\nonto the previously delivered frame. Intermediate reducer emits may be\nconflated for a slow observer; the full row set is never redelivered\n(full-set redelivery is the O(rows squared) P0 #485 exists to kill).\n- Windowed (`window` is `Some`): `window.rows` is the complete current\nbounded set and `deltas` is ALWAYS empty -- bridges replace state from\nthe snapshot, so shipping deltas too would cross every row the FFI\nboundary twice just to be folded and discarded.",
    ),
}

    ),
}

        "Immutable NIP-01 event body accepted by the governed sign-only operation.\nThe author is deliberately absent and is frozen from engine identity state.",
    ),
}

record Record {
    name: "FfiWriteIntent",
    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
    remote: false,
    fields: [
        Field {
            name: "payload",
            type_: Enum {
                module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                name: "FfiWritePayload",
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
        Field {
            name: "durability",
            type_: Enum {
                module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                name: "FfiDurability",
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
        Field {
            name: "routing",
            type_: Enum {
                module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                name: "FfiWriteRouting",
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: None,
        },
        Field {
            name: "identity_override",
            type_: Optional {
                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: Some(
                None,
            ),
            docstring: Some(
                "`nmp::WriteIntent::identity_override` mirror (#47 Unit A): the\nidentity this ONE write is published under, as 64-char hex (the\nmodule-wide `convert::parse_pubkey` rule every other pubkey input\nhere follows) or bech32 `npub` (an identity is the one input an app\nmost plausibly holds in display form). `None` -- the default, and\nwhat every existing caller gets -- means \"the active account at\nacceptance time\", unchanged.\n`Some` must name exactly the payload's own author; misuse fails\nclosed, never silently retargets: a MALFORMED string is a typed\nsynchronous [`crate::convert::FfiError::InvalidPublicKey`] before any\nengine call, while a well-formed-but-MISMATCHED override (not the\ndraft/event author) is rejected at the engine's acceptance boundary\nas `FfiWriteStatus::Failed` on the receipt stream -- no `Accepted`\never precedes it. An override naming a pubkey with no registered\nsigner parks as `FfiWriteStatus::AwaitingCapability` (retained, not\nterminated) until that capability attaches; acceptance PINS the\noverride, so a later `set_active_account` cannot retarget the write.",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "correlation",
            type_: Optional {
                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: Some(
                None,
            ),
            docstring: Some(
                "`nmp_grammar::WriteIntent::correlation` mirror (#591): a caller-\ngenerated crash-safe correlation/idempotency token. `None` -- the\ndefault -- opts this write out of correlation entirely. `Some`\ncrosses the boundary as a plain string and is validated by\n`nmp_grammar::CorrelationToken`'s `TryFrom<&str>` on the way in\n(non-empty, length-capped): a malformed token is a typed synchronous\n[`crate::convert::FfiError::InvalidCorrelationToken`] before any\nengine call. A token that already resolves to a previously-accepted\nreceipt reattaches that existing obligation instead of enqueuing a\nsecond write -- see that type's doc for the full contract.",
            ),
        },
    ],
    docstring: Some(
        "A caller's publish request (`nmp::WriteIntent` mirror).",
    ),
}

record Record {
    name: "NmpEngineConfig",
    module_path: "nmp_ffi",

    fields: [
        Field {
            name: "store_path",
            type_: Optional {
                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: Some(
                "`None` -> in-memory store (nothing survives a restart). `Some(path)`\n-> a persistent `RedbStore` opened at that path (the same file\nreopened across restarts is what preserves source-scoped evidence for\na cold, offline read -- ledger #7).",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "indexer_relays",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: String,
            },

        },

                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: Some(
                "Operator app relay set (`Lane::AppRelay`). Default empty.",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "fallback_relays",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: None,
            docstring: Some(
                "Operator fallback relay set (`Lane::Fallback`). Default empty.",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "allowed_local_relay_hosts",
            type_: Sequence {
                inner_type: String,
            },
            default: Some(
                EmptySequence,
            ),
            docstring: Some(
                "Local/private relay HOSTS the operator explicitly opts into despite\nthe SSRF admission policy (issue #121). A DISCOVERED (network-sourced\nkind:10002) relay on a loopback / RFC-1918 / link-local / `.onion`\nhost is rejected by default; listing its host here (e.g. `\"127.0.0.1\"`\nor `\"localhost\"`) re-admits discovered relays on that exact host.\nHost-only match (port- and path-insensitive). Default empty.\n\n`default = []` keeps this field OPTIONAL for existing foreign-language\ncallers — adding it must not break records constructed before #121.",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "max_relays",
            type_: UInt32,
            default: Some(
                UInt(
                    10,
                    Decimal,
                    UInt32,
                ),
            ),
            docstring: Some(
                "The one whole-engine relay ceiling. It bounds the complete compiled\ndemand and the transport worker set with the same effective value.\nLegacy zero is normalized to the finite default, never uncapped.\n\nThe `default =` literal below MUST stay equal to\n[`DEFAULT_MAX_RELAYS`] (uniffi record defaults accept only a literal,\nnever a const path) — the const is the single Rust-side knob; the\nliteral is its foreign-binding mirror.",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "max_native_tasks",
            type_: UInt32,
            default: Some(
                UInt(
                    12,
                    Decimal,
                    UInt32,
                ),
            ),
            docstring: Some(
                "Maximum immediately-running native observer/action/waiter tasks.\nZero selects the finite default; saturation never queues an accepted\nstream behind a long-lived drain.",
            ),
        },
        Field {
            name: "max_auth_capabilities",
            type_: UInt32,
            default: Some(
                UInt(
                    64,
                    Decimal,
                    UInt32,
                ),
            ),
            docstring: Some(
                "Maximum live signer and AUTH-policy registrations. Zero deliberately\nadmits none rather than selecting the default.",
            ),
        },
    ],
    docstring: Some(
        "Construction config for [`NmpEngine::new`]. See the module doc: the only\nrelay facts a caller ever supplies are the three operator-configured\nlanes -- `indexer_relays`, `app_relays`, `fallback_relays`\n(`routing-and-ownership.md` §2.1) -- everything else is discovered live.",
    ),
}

    name: "decode_remembered_groups",
    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
    is_async: false,
    arguments: [
        Argument {
            name: "row",
            type_: Record {
                module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                name: "FfiRow",
            },
            by_ref: false,
            optional: false,
            default: None,

Opaque and generically named -- a future protocol\nmodule's own composed intent could reuse this same wrapper shape,\nnothing here is NIP-29-specific except how it's constructed.\nTake-once, not `Clone`/re-readable: [`NmpEngine::publish_composed`]\n(`crate::facade`) takes the inner intent exactly once, and a second\ncall fails closed with [`FfiError::IntentAlreadyConsumed`] rather than\nsilently re-publishing a stale template or handing back nothing.",
    ),
}

            docstring: Some(
                "Open a live subscription. `observer` is driven from a dedicated drain\nthread (M4 plan §4b) -- never the engine thread itself. The returned\n[`NmpQueryHandle`]'s `Drop` withdraws the subscription (deinit-tied\ndemand drop, plan §4c); call [`NmpQueryHandle::cancel`] for an\nexplicit early teardown instead of waiting on Swift's own `deinit`\ntiming.\n\n`window` selects the observation's delivery policy (#485). `None` is\ntoday's unbounded observation: exact deltas are rebased when a slow\nobserver skips intermediate reducer emits, and the full set is never\nredelivered. `Some(FfiWindow::Expandable { initial, max })` is a\nbounded newest-first window: each frame carries the complete\ncurrent row set + growth fact in `FfiFrame::window` (deltas stay\nempty on the wire) and grows only via\n[`NmpQueryHandle::request_rows`], never above `max`. Zero bounds and\n`initial > max` fail closed here with a typed [`FfiError`]; a\nwindowed selection that already declares a NIP-01 `limit` fails with\n[`FfiError::WindowSelectionHasLimit`].",
            ),
            throws: Some(
                Enum {
                    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
                    name: "FfiError",
                },
            ),

            checksum: Some(
                58546,

Growth outcomes arrive as\n[`crate::types::FfiWindowLoad`] facts in delivered frames -- reaching\nthe declared `max` is the `AtBound` FACT there, never an error here.\nUnbounded observations fail with\n[`FfiRequestRowsError::Unwindowed`].",

    docstring: Some(
        "The app-facing handle to a live subscription (returned by\n[`NmpEngine::observe`]). `Drop` withdraws the subscription -- the SDK\nnever requires an app-owned container or lifecycle hook to make this\nhappen (plan §7's kill test). Holds ONLY two opaque capabilities: the\n[`nmp::ObservationCancel`] token (`Subscription::cancel_handle`) and,\nfor windowed observations, the [`nmp::WindowHandle`] growth capability\n-- no `Handle`/`QueryHandle` (the raw imperative engine-control\ncapability) ever reaches this crate. The receiving half of the\nsubscription is owned entirely by [`NmpEngine::observe`]'s drain thread,\nsince `recv()` blocks; `cancel()`/`Drop` here and the drain thread's own\nteardown converge on the token's single withdrawal guard (see that\ntype's doc).",
    ),
}

Because the underlying mailbox is latest-wins\n(see `nmp::DiagnosticsSubscription::recv`'s doc), a slow Swift-side\nconsumer may observe fewer snapshots than were actually produced, but\nnever a stale one out of order. `on_closed` fires exactly once, when the\nobserver is cancelled or the engine shuts down.",
    ),
}

    },
    docstring: Some(
        "Drains a publish's `Receiver<WriteStatus>` (ledger #9: enqueue is not\nconverged -- this may be called many times per publish, ending only when\nthe intent reaches every relay's terminal, or never at all for an\n`Ephemeral` intent, mirroring `Handle::publish`'s own receiver).\n`on_closed` fires exactly once, when the receipt's `Sender` is dropped\n(the intent has resolved or the engine has shut down) -- after which no\nfurther `on_status` call will ever occur, mirroring `RowObserver` and\n`DiagnosticsObserver`.",
    ),
}

callback CallbackInterface {
    name: "RowObserver",
    module_path: "nmp_ffi",
    methods: [
        Method {
            name: "on_frame",
            object_name: "RowObserver",

                Argument {

                    optional: false,
                    default: None,
                },
            ],

                name: "uniffi_nmp_ffi_fn_method_rowobserver_on_frame",

                is_object_free_function: false,
            },
            docstring: None,

                46105,
            ),
        },
        Method {
            name: "on_closed",
            object_name: "RowObserver",

            is_async: false,
            object_impl: Struct,
            arguments: [],
            return_type: None,
            ffi_func: FfiFunction {
                name: "uniffi_nmp_ffi_fn_method_rowobserver_on_closed",
                is_async: false,
                arguments: [],
                return_type: None,
                has_rust_call_status_arg: true,
                is_object_free_function: false,
            },
            docstring: None,
            throws: None,
            takes_self_by_arc: false,
            checksum_fn_name: "uniffi_nmp_ffi_checksum_method_rowobserver_on_closed",
            checksum: Some(
                36135,
            ),
        },
    ],
    ffi_init_callback: FfiFunction {
        name: "uniffi_nmp_ffi_fn_init_callback_vtable_rowobserver",
        is_async: false,
        arguments: [
            FfiArgument {
                name: "vtable",
                type_: Reference(
                    Struct(
                        "VTableCallbackInterfaceRowObserver",
                    ),
                ),
            },
        ],
        return_type: None,
        has_rust_call_status_arg: false,
        is_object_free_function: false,
    },
    docstring: Some(
        "Drains a live subscription's frames (M4 §4b) -- the ONE observer both\nobservation modes share (#485): unbounded frames carry the exact delta\ntransition, windowed frames carry the complete bounded row set plus its\ngrowth fact (see [`FfiFrame`]'s doc for why delivery derives from\nboundedness and rows never cross the wire twice). `on_frame` is called\nonce per delivered frame, in order, on a dedicated drain thread — never\non the engine thread itself, so a slow native consumer cannot stall\n`EngineCore`'s own recv loop. Both delivery modes conflate under\nbackpressure: windowed frames keep the latest complete snapshot and\nunbounded frames compose one exact transition rebased onto the last\ndelivered state, so a slow consumer sees fewer intermediate frames but\nits next frame still reaches newest state. `on_closed` fires exactly once, when the engine\nhas torn the subscription down (cancel, or the frame channel's `Sender`\nwas dropped for any other reason) — after which no further `on_frame`\ncall will ever occur. `frame.evidence` is the query's scoped per-source\nacquisition evidence (`docs/design/scoped-evidence-49-12-plan.md` §4) --\nnever a collapsed completeness verdict.",
    ),
}
